name: WorkLoop
description:
Library component. Models the polecat's work loop encountering a gate-blocking step. When this loop observes a gate in Open state, it fires a Park signal to the polecat. Single-shot.

Real gastown analog: the polecat process running its agent prompt loop hits a `bd gate wait <id>` (or equivalent) call. The work step's effect is to halt forward progress and emit a Park signal. From the substrate's perspective, the polecat is now Parked and waiting for the gate's external dependency to resolve.

Used by gate-3587 specs to make the parking event modeled-by-cause rather than baked-in. Replaces the earlier `Parker` stub which fired Park unconditionally at t=0; with WorkLoop, the Park message is conditional on observing Gate=Open, demonstrating that parking is a *consequence* of the gate state, not an initial assumption. See gate-3587-defense.md P1.2.

GATE_OBSERVE.description: observe the gate that the work loop encounters.

PARK_OUT.description: fires the Park signal at the bound polecat. Tagged 'Park'.

actors.name: WorkLoop
actors.owner: Polecat work-loop step that hits a gate. Pending -> Sent on observation that gate is Open.

triples.event: work step encounters open gate; emit Park signal to polecat
